matches.match_rationale: Campus spreadsheets arrive in inconsistent formats with varying schema definitions (e.g., 'active enrollment' means different things at different sites). AI-assisted ETL can normalize field names, resolve definitional conflicts, and map disparate inputs to a canonical schema automatically. This directly addresses the core pain of conflicting numbers from the same campus.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI transformation layer that ingests campus spreadsheet uploads, detects schema variants, flags definitional mismatches (e.g., part-time vs. full-time enrollment counts), and outputs a standardized consolidated dataset with a reconciliation log.

matches.match_rationale: HQ receiving conflicting numbers from the same campus — or numbers that deviate significantly from prior periods — is an anomaly detection problem. AI can flag statistically improbable values, sudden enrollment spikes, or inter-campus outliers before they enter consolidated reports. This replaces manual spot-checking which currently fails to catch errors.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated data validation layer that scores each campus submission for statistical anomalies and cross-report inconsistencies, surfacing a daily 'data quality alert' to the HQ reporting team before consolidation runs.

matches.match_rationale: The end-to-end reporting cycle — collecting submissions, chasing late campuses, validating inputs, resolving conflicts, consolidating, and distributing — is a multi-step workflow currently done manually. A task automation agent can orchestrate this entire pipeline: ingest, validate, flag issues, request corrections from campus contacts, and publish a reconciled report with an audit trail.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A reporting automation agent that runs on a weekly cadence: collects campus data files, runs validation checks, emails campus contacts with specific correction requests for flagged anomalies, and produces a consolidated report with full audit trail once all issues resolve.

matches.match_rationale: Once clean consolidated data exists, HQ leadership still needs narrative reports that explain variances across campuses. AI can auto-generate the written commentary — explaining why enrollment at Campus A differs from Campus B, summarizing data quality issues resolved in this cycle, and producing board-ready summaries from structured data.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated narrative generator that takes consolidated campus metrics and produces a structured executive briefing with variance explanations, flagged data quality exceptions, and trend commentary — reducing the analyst time needed to write reports from hours to minutes.
